title: Per-Origin Politeness, Token Timing, Retry Budgets, and Fairness · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Track per-origin active requests, last start, next eligibility, request and byte budgets, robots state, and owner override.
Enforce minimum spacing between request starts with monotonic time and avoid holding shared locks while sleeping.
Classify transport and HTTP failures by method safety, response evidence, attempt, elapsed, and crawl budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Origin State
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 8106 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track per-origin active requests, last start, next eligibility, request and byte budgets, robots state, and owner override.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Origin Fairness has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Origin Fairness, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Origin Fairness precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Origin Fairness, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 8902 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Origin Fairness, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One FIFO channel is fair across origins.

options.text: Declare Authorized site auditing and Go method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the debug Origin Fairness case
Predict Authorized site auditing and Go resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the debug Origin Fairness case
Run the smallest deterministic Authorized site auditing and Go request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the debug Origin Fairness case
Inject one changed Authorized site auditing and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the debug Origin Fairness case

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Origin Fairness
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-origin-fairness-2lc1wo" that uses case 5129 to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Origin Fairness.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Origin Fairness into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Origin Fairness, keep Origin Fairness connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Origin Fairness
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Origin Fairness: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 5664 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Origin Fairness.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Origin Fairness, keep Origin Fairness connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.
